title: Full-text search on task descriptions regressed to 10-15s after v2.3 upgrade
problem: After upgrading from TaskFlow v2.2 to v2.3 approximately two weeks ago, full-text search across task descriptions takes 10-15 seconds to return results. Search by title remains fast. The user has ~5,000 tasks accumulated over 2 years, some with lengthy descriptions containing pasted meeting notes.
root_cause_hypothesis: The v2.3 release likely introduced a regression in the full-text search indexing or query strategy for task descriptions. Possible causes: (1) an FTS index on the description column was dropped or not migrated, (2) the search query was changed from an indexed full-text search to an unindexed pattern match (LIKE/ILIKE), or (3) a new search feature in v2.3 added unindexed fields to the search scope. The fact that title search remains fast while description search is slow points directly at description-specific indexing — titles are short enough to scan quickly even without an index, but large descriptions (~5K rows with lengthy text) would be severely impacted by a missing or broken FTS index.
reproduction_steps: Set up a TaskFlow v2.3 instance with ~5,000 tasks, some with lengthy descriptions (paragraph-length or longer)
Perform a full-text search using the global search targeting task descriptions
Compare with the same search on a v2.2 instance with equivalent data — expect significantly faster results
Additionally, compare description search time vs title-only search time on v2.3 to confirm the discrepancy
environment: TaskFlow v2.3 (upgraded from v2.2), running on a work laptop, ~5,000 tasks with some containing lengthy descriptions (pasted meeting notes)
impact: Users with moderate-to-large task databases experience unacceptable search latency (10-15s) on description searches, making the full-text search feature effectively unusable for daily workflows. Title search is unaffected, so users are not fully blocked but lose access to a key capability.
recommended_fix: 1. Diff the v2.2→v2.3 changes to search-related code (query construction, indexing, migrations). 2. Check whether the description FTS index exists in a v2.3 database — run EXPLAIN/EXPLAIN ANALYZE on the description search query to confirm whether it's doing a sequential scan. 3. If an index was dropped, add a migration to restore it. If the query strategy changed, revert or optimize the description search path. 4. Benchmark with a dataset of ~5K tasks with large descriptions to verify the fix brings response time back to sub-second.
proposed_test_case: Create a performance regression test that seeds a database with 5,000 tasks (with realistic-length descriptions averaging 500+ words) and asserts that a full-text description search completes within an acceptable threshold (e.g., under 2 seconds). Run this test against both v2.2 and v2.3 schemas to catch indexing regressions.
information_gaps: Exact database backend in use (SQLite, PostgreSQL, etc.) — though the investigation path is the same regardless
Whether the user is running self-hosted or cloud-hosted TaskFlow
Whether the v2.3 upgrade ran all migrations successfully
